text: Light Filters are used to manipulate lighting results. In the real world, stencils and gels often achieve this by changing the color, quality, and amount of light in a shot. RenderMan's light filters are a powerful and flexible way to do the same (and even more) in lighting your scenes.
Working with Light Filters in Solaris is simple and will instantly transform your lights and lighting!
To add a light filter simply add one of the dedicated LOP node to create the corresponding USD light filter primitive.
LOP, while still working, is deprecated.
Then assign it to a light using one of the following methods:
Use a PxrAssign LOP (or the PxrAssignLightFilter alias). You can use a single PxrAssign LOP to assign multiple light filters to multiple lights.
Use a Light Edit LOP and fill the
Plase a Light Edit LOP after the light filter LOP and fill the
If the assignment was properly done, in the Scene Graph, on the light primitive, the light filter primitive should be listed on the light:filters attribute.
To parent a light filter to a light (as shown above in the Scene Graph), simply set the
parameter on the light filter LOP with the proper path (e.g. ‘/lights/light1/$OS instead of the default '/lightFilters/$OS’).
